user_task: Nemo Retriever library setup on local CPU-only machine: no GPU detected (automatic detection) — configuration via build.nvidia.com (requires only an API key). Embedding, reranking, extraction reachable.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, SETUP bucket, row 1; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, SETUP → 'Setup on local CPU-only machine — hosted endpoints via build.nvidia.com'.

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — (1) clean library install, (2) ingest a PDF hitting hosted endpoints, (3) a retrieval query returns grounded hits, all binary pass/fail
success_criteria.time: end-to-end (clean state → first successful retrieval) ≤ 10 minutes
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a setup-and-load-my-docs prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must use `retriever ingest` then `retriever query` with CPU/hosted-appropriate flags (api-key + hosted endpoints), NOT local-GPU flags

environment.note: On a CPU-only host the extraction NIM invoke_urls resolve to the hosted ai.api.nvidia.com defaults when left unset; the API key is read from NVIDIA_API_KEY when --api-key is omitted. Embedding runs on the bundled CPU HuggingFace model by default; pin --embed-invoke-url https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1 to force the hosted embedder (the 'hosted models' intent of this task).

cases.title: Smoke loop: install check + single text-PDF ingest → query
cases.complexity_axis: Baseline happy path: one trivial 1-page text PDF, no special flags. Proves the install is on PATH and the ingest→query loop closes on a no-GPU host.
cases.prompt: I'm on a Mac with no GPU. Set me up to run document Q&A using NVIDIA's hosted models, then confirm it works: load data/t1_smoke/test.pdf and tell me what the document says.

expected_output.version: prints a version string like 2026.06.10.devXXXXXXXX (CLI is on PATH)
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each hit has keys {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit
expected_output.answer: Document contains lines of placeholder text and references an image; cite source=...test.pdf page_number=1

pass_when: retriever --version (or equivalent) succeeds — install present, no GPU required
`retriever ingest` runs against the file and writes rows to lancedb/nemo-retriever (api key resolved from NVIDIA_API_KEY)
`retriever query` returns >= 1 hit citing test.pdf p1
no local-GPU-only flags used (no --*-gpus-per-actor / GPU backends)

cases.title: Prove CPU/hosted routing via --dry-run, then real ingest → query
cases.complexity_axis: Adds an explicit verification step before any billable call: the agent must reason about the resolved plan (--dry-run) to confirm hosted endpoints + API key + no GPU, then run for real. Exercises validation-path bullets 'NVIDIA_API_KEY discovered' and 'No GPU detected; no CUDA deps'.
cases.prompt: Before loading anything, prove my setup will use NVIDIA's hosted endpoints and my API key — not a local GPU. Then load data/t2_hosted_plan/woods_frost.pdf and tell me who owns the woods.

dry_run_plan_assertions: embed.gpu_embed == null AND batch_tuning.gpu_* all null (no GPU reserved)
vdb_upload.vdb_kwargs == {uri: lancedb, table_name: nemo-retriever, overwrite: true}
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.answer: The woods' owner's 'house is in the village'; i.e. the owner lives in the village. cite woods_frost.pdf p1

pass_when: agent runs --dry-run and confirms hosted embedding endpoint + null GPU fields BEFORE the real ingest
real ingest + query succeed; answer grounded in woods_frost.pdf p1
subcommand accuracy: --dry-run used for inspection, dropped for the real run

cases.title: Hosted extraction stack: table-bearing PDF → cell lookup
cases.complexity_axis: Moves beyond plain text+embed: ingest now drives the full hosted extraction stack (page-elements → OCR v2 → table-structure), all on ai.api.nvidia.com. Tests the 'extraction reachable' clause of the task. Query must surface a specific table cell.
cases.prompt: On my no-GPU box, load data/t3_table/table_test.pdf and tell me what value James had in 2019.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.note: Default ingest enables extract_tables + use_page_elements + ocr_version v2; on CPU these route to hosted NIMs.

pass_when: ingest completes with table rows present (hosted extraction stack reachable)
query returns the correct cell value (978) citing table_test.pdf p1
if --content-types is used it is text,table (not an unsupported value)

cases.title: Folder ingest + hosted reranker
cases.complexity_axis: Two new dimensions at once: (a) folder-level ingest of 3 mixed PDFs in one invocation, and (b) --rerank, which exercises the third hosted model class (reranker) and the 'best match first' ordering. Covers the 'reranking reachable' clause.
cases.prompt: Set up on CPU with hosted models, load the whole data/t4_folder/ folder, and find — best match first — which Robert Frost collection was published in 1923.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3)
expected_output.answer: New Hampshire (1923); cite woods_frost.pdf p2 (collection table)
expected_output.rerank_note: --rerank with no URL on a CPU host resolves to the hosted reranker https://ai.api.nvidia.com/.../llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2/reranking; reranked order differs from dense-only order

pass_when: single `retriever ingest` call on the folder ingests all 3 PDFs (no per-file loop)
query uses --rerank and returns the correct collection (New Hampshire) as/among the top hits, citing woods_frost.pdf p2
reranker leg reachable (no rerank endpoint error)

cases.title: Full operational-pass acceptance: fresh named index, citations, no-GPU + ≤ 10 min
cases.complexity_axis: Composed acceptance gate for the whole row: a clean end-to-end run into a custom-named index (--table-name must stay aligned across ingest AND query), citation required in the answer, explicit assertion that no local GPU was used (hosted-only trace), and the ≤ 10 min end-to-end SLA. This is the gating test the others build up to.
cases.prompt: Do a clean end-to-end setup on this no-GPU machine using only my NVIDIA API key and hosted models: load data/t5_acceptance/ into a fresh index called 'setup_smoke', then answer with a citation — in which poem does the speaker say they have 'miles to go before I sleep'? Confirm no local GPU was used and that it finished in under 10 minutes.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/t5_acceptance/ --lancedb-uri lancedb --table-name setup_smoke --embed-invoke-url https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1 --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2
$RETRIEVER query "miles to go before I sleep" --table-name setup_smoke --rerank --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/setup_smoke.
expected_output.answer: 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' by Robert Frost; cite woods_frost.pdf p1
assertions: query reads the SAME table (setup_smoke) the ingest wrote — not the default nemo-retriever
every returned hit carries non-null source + page_number
trace shows only ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com calls; no CUDA / local-GPU model load
wall clock (clean state → query answer) <= 10 min

pass_when: ingest writes to lancedb/setup_smoke and query reads from setup_smoke (table names aligned)
answer correct + citation (woods_frost.pdf p1) present on the result
no GPU used (hosted endpoints only); end-to-end <= 10 min
